ICCV 15, pages 19 – 27, USA. IEEE Computer So-
ciety. A
Appendix
A.1
Detailed Results
Table 7 and Table 8 show detailed results on the
Context Association Test for the development and
test sets respectively. A.2
Mechanical Turk Task
Our crowdworkers were required to have a 95%
HIT acceptance rate, and be located in the United
States. In total, 475 and 803 annotators completed
the intrasentence and intersentence tasks respec-
tively. Restricting crowdworkers to the United
States helps account for differing deﬁnitions of
stereotypes based on regional social expectations,
though limitations in the dataset remain as dis-
cussed in Section 9. Screenshots of our Mechani-
cal Turk interface are available in Figure 2 and 3. A.3
Target Words
Table 9 list our target terms used in the dataset col-
lection task. A.4
General Methods for Training a Next
Sentence Prediction Head
Given some context c, and some sentence s, our
intersentence task requires calculating the likeli-
hood p(s|c), for some sentence s and context sen-
tence c.
While BERT has been trained with a Next
Sentence Prediction classiﬁcation head to provide
p(s|c), the other models have not. In this section,
we detail our creation of a Next Sentence Predic-
tion classiﬁcation head as a downstream task. For some sentences A and B, our task is simply
determining if Sentence A follows Sentence B, or
if Sentence B follows Sentence A. We trivially
generate this corpus from Wikipedia by sampling
some ith sentence, i + 1th sentence, and a ran-
domly chosen negative sentence from any other
article. We maintain a maximum sequence length
of 256 tokens, and our training set consists of 9.5
million examples. We train with a batch size of 80 sequences until
convergence (80 sequences / batch * 256 tokens
/ sequence = 20,480 tokens/batch) for 10 epochs
over the corpus. For BERT, We use BertAdam as
the optimizer, with a learning rate of 1e-5, a linear
warmup schedule from 50 steps to 500 steps, and
minimize cross entropy for our loss function. Our
results are comparable to Devlin et al. (2019), with
each model obtaining 93-98% accuracy against the
test set of 3.5 million examples. Additional models maintain the same experi-
mental details. Our NSP classiﬁer achieves an
94.6% accuracy with roberta-base, a 97.1%
accuracy with roberta-large, a93.4% accu-
racy with xlnet-base and 94.1% accuracy with
xlnet-large. In order to evaluate GPT-2 on intersentence
tasks, we feed the mean-pooled representations
across the entire sequence length into the clas-
siﬁcation head. Our NSP classiﬁer obtains a
92.5% accuracy on gpt2-small, 94.2% on
gpt2-medium, and 96.1% on gpt2-large. In order to ﬁne-tune gpt2-large on our ma-
chines, we utilized gradient accumulation with a
step size of 10, and mixed precision training from
Apex. A.5
Fine-Tuning BERT for Sentiment
Analysis
In order to evaluate sentiment, we ﬁne-tune BERT
(Devlin et al., 2019) on movie reviews (Maas et al.,
2011) for seven epochs. We used a maximum se-
quence length of 256 WordPieces, batch size 32,
and used Adam with a learning rate of 1e−4. Our
ﬁne-tuned model achieves an 92% test accuracy
on the Large Movie Review dataset.

H1: REFERENCES
REFERENCE: ON-MEASURING-SOCIAL-BIASES-IN-SENTENCE-ENCODERS
  PROCEEDINGS=NAC-ACL; PAGES=622–628; LOCATION=MINNEAPOLIS,MN; PUBLISHER=ACL
REFERENCE: MIKOLOV ET AL.-2013: DISTRIBUTED-REPRESENTATIONS-OF-WORDS-AND-PHRASES ∧ COMPOSITIONALITY
  PROCEEDINGS=NEURIPS13; PAGES=3111–3119; LOCATION=RED-HOOK,NY,USA; PUBLISHER=CURRAN
REFERENCE: NOSEK BANAJI GREENWALD-2002: MATH=MALE, ME=FEMALE; ∴ MATH≠ME

REFERENCE: PENNINGTON SOCHER MANNING-2014: GLOVE:GLOBAL-VECTORS-FOR-WORD-REPRESENTATION
  PROCEEDINGS=EMNLP; PAGES=1532–1543
REFERENCE: PETERS ET AL.-2018: DEEP-CONTEXTUALIZED-WORD-REPRESENTATIONS
  PROCEEDINGS=NAC-ACL; PAGES=2227–2237; PUBLISHER=ACL
REFERENCE: RADFORD ET AL.-2019: LANGUAGE-MODELS ARE UNSUPERVISED-MULTITASK-LEARNERS
  SOURCE=OPENAI-BLOG; ISSUE=1(8)
REFERENCE: RUDINGER ET AL.-2018: GENDER-BIAS-IN-COREFERENCE-RESOLUTION
  PROCEEDINGS=NAACL; PAGES=8–14
REFERENCE: SHENG ET AL.-2019: THE-WOMAN-WORKED-AS-A-BABYSITTER:ON-BIASES-IN-LANGUAGE-GENERATION
  PROCEEDINGS=EMNLP-IJCNLP; PAGES=3407–3412; LOCATION=HONG-KONG,CHINA; PUBLISHER=ACL
REFERENCE: VRANDECIK KRÖTZSCH-2014: WIKIDATA:FREE-COLLABORATIVE-KNOWLEDGEBASE
  JOURNAL=COMM-ACM; VOLUME=57(10); PAGES=78–85
REFERENCE: YANG ET AL.-2019: XLNET:GENERALIZED-AUTOREGRESSIVE-PRETRAINING-FOR-LANGUAGE-UNDERSTANDING
  PROCEEDINGS=NEURIPS; PAGES=5753–5763; EDITORS=WALLACH-ET-AL; PUBLISHER=CURRAN
REFERENCE: ZHAO ET AL.-2018: GENDER-BIAS-IN-COREFERENCE-RESOLUTION:EVALUATION-AND-DEBIASING-METHODS
  PROCEEDINGS=NAC-ACL; PAGES=15–20
REFERENCE: ZHU ET AL.-2015: ALIGNING-BOOKS-AND-MOVIES:TOWARDS-STORY-LIKE-VISUAL-EXPLANATIONS
  PROCEEDINGS=ICCV15; PAGES=19–27; PUBLISHER=IEEE-COMPUTER-SOCIETY

H1: APPENDIX
H2: A.1 DETAILED-RESULTS
  RESULTS: TABLE7 ∧ TABLE8 SHOW DETAILED-RESULTS ON CONTEXT-ASSOCIATION-TEST FOR {DEV; TEST}
H2: A.2 MECHANICAL-TURK-TASK
  TASK: CROWDWORKERS REQUIRE HIT-ACCEPTANCE≥95% ∧ LOCATION=US
  TASK: COMPLETED={INTRASENTENCE:475; INTERSENTENCE:803}
  NOTE: US-RESTRICTION ACCOUNTS FOR REGIONAL-STEREOTYPE-DEFINITIONS ∧ LIMITATIONS REMAIN (SECTION9)
  NOTE: INTERFACE-SCREENSHOTS IN FIGURES2–3
H2: A.3 TARGET-WORDS
  NOTE: TABLE9 LISTS TARGET-TERMS FOR DATASET-COLLECTION
H2: A.4 NSP-HEAD-TRAINING-METHODS
  CTX: NSP-TASK
    STEP1: FOR CONTEXT=c ∧ SENTENCE=s COMPUTE p(s|c)
    NOTE: BERT-INCLUDES NSP-HEAD ∧ OTHERS DO NOT
    CORPUS-GEN: SAMPLE {SENTENCE_i; SENTENCE_{i+1}} POSITIVE ∧ RANDOM NEGATIVE FROM OTHER-WIKI-ARTICLE
    PARAM: MAX-SEQUENCE-LENGTH=256 TOKENS
    DATASET=9.5×10^6 EXAMPLES
    TRAIN: BATCH=80-SEQUENCES; EPOCHS=10 (80 SEQ/BATCH×256 TOK/SEQ=20,480 TOK/BATCH)
    OPTIMIZER=BERTADAM; LR=1e-5 ∧ WARMUP=50→500 STEPS; LOSS=CROSS-ENTROPY
    RESULT(BERT)=ACC=93–98% @ TEST=3.5×10^6 EXAMPLES
    RESULTS: ROBERTA-BASE=94.6%; ROBERTA-LARGE=97.1%; XLNET-BASE=93.4%; XLNET-LARGE=94.1%
H2: A.4.1 GPT2-NSP-EVALUATION
  METHOD: MEAN-POOL SEQUENCE REPRESENTATIONS → CLASSIFICATION-HEAD
  RESULTS: GPT2-SMALL=92.5%; GPT2-MEDIUM=94.2%; GPT2-LARGE=96.1%
  FINE-TUNE: GPT2-LARGE WITH GRADIENT-ACCUMULATION STEP-SIZE=10 ∧ MIXED-PRECISION(APEX)
H2: A.5 FINE-TUNING-BERT-SENTIMENT-ANALYSIS
  METHOD: FINE-TUNE BERT(DEVLIN:2019) ON MOVIE-REVIEWS(MAAS:2011) FOR EPOCHS=7
  PARAM: MAX-SEQUENCE-LENGTH=256-WORDPIECES; BATCH=32; OPTIMIZER=ADAM; LR=1e-4
  RESULT=ACC=92% @ LARGE-MOVIE-REVIEW TEST-SET
